name: Irish Brown Bread#1 Monica Sheridan
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Stone Ground Whole Wheat Flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the whole wheat flour thoroughly with the white flour, salt, and baking soda.
2. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and gradually add the liquid. Stir with a wooden spoon until combined. You may need less or more liquid depending on the absorbency of the flour. The dough should be soft but manageable.
3. Knead the dough into a ball in the mixing bowl using your floured hands.
4. Place the dough on a lightly floured baking sheet and flatten it into a circle that is 1 1/2 inches thick with the palm of your hand.
5. Using a knife dipped in flour, make a cross through the center of the bread so it will easily break into quarters when baked.
6. Bake at 425°F for 25 minutes. Reduce the temperature to 350°F and bake for an additional 15 minutes.
7. If the crust is too hard, wrap the baked bread in a damp tea cloth. Allow the loaf to stand upright until cool.
8. Do not cut the bread until it has set, about 6 hours after it comes out of the oven.
